title: Kotlin Reflection
content:
### Reflection in Kotlin

Reflection is the ability to inspect and interact with code at runtime. Kotlin provides its own reflection API alongside Java reflection.

---

### Kotlin Reflection Dependency

```kotlin
// build.gradle.kts
implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlin:kotlin-reflect:1.9.0")
```

---

### Class References

```kotlin
val strClass = String::class
val personClass = Person::class

println(strClass.simpleName)  // String
println(strClass.qualifiedName) // kotlin.String
println(strClass.isAbstract)  // false
```

---

### Member Properties and Functions

```kotlin
data class Person(val name: String, var age: Int)

fun main() {
    val person = Person("Alice", 30)
    val kClass = person::class
    
    // Properties
    for (prop in kClass.memberProperties) {
        println("${prop.name} = ${prop.get(person)}")
    }
    
    // Functions
    for (func in kClass.memberFunctions) {
        println("Function: ${func.name}")
    }
}
```

---

### Callable References

```kotlin
fun isPositive(x: Int) = x > 0

fun main() {
    val predicate: (Int) -> Boolean = ::isPositive
    
    println(predicate(5))   // true
    println(predicate(-3))  // false
    
    val numbers = listOf(-2, -1, 0, 1, 2)
    println(numbers.filter(::isPositive))  // [1, 2]
}
```

---

### Property References

```kotlin
data class Person(val name: String, var age: Int)

fun main() {
    val person = Person("Alice", 30)
    
    val nameProp = Person::name
    val ageProp = Person::age
    
    println(nameProp.get(person))  // Alice
    ageProp.set(person, 31)
    println(person.age)  // 31
}
```

---

### Modifying Properties via Reflection

```kotlin
fun setProperty(obj: Any, propertyName: String, value: Any?) {
    val prop = obj::class.memberProperties
        .firstOrNull { it.name == propertyName && it is KMutableProperty<*> }
    
    if (prop is KMutableProperty<*>) {
        prop.setter.call(obj, value)
    }
}
```

---

### Constructor References

```kotlin
class Person(val name: String, val age: Int)

fun main() {
    val factory: (String, Int) -> Person = ::Person
    
    val alice = factory("Alice", 30)
    println(alice)  // Person@...
}
```

> **Key Point:** Reflection allows runtime inspection of types. Prefer callable references (`::`) for type-safe reflection. Add `kotlin-reflect` dependency for full Kotlin reflection support.

quiz.question: How do you get a class reference in Kotlin?
What is the operator for callable references?
